title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Pipeline Repair Bay
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective versioned reusable automation contract; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in reusable workflows, composite actions, interfaces, and permission flow, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned reusable automation contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Reuse Component Selection
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.

Inspect the Ci Reuse Component Selection trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Reuse Component Selection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose reusable workflow, composite action, javascript action, docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Reuse Component Selection, keep Ci Reuse Component Selection connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Ci Reuse Component Selection, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ci Reuse Component Selection, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Shared Automation Evolution
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-shared-automation-evolution-18e1ao". Tailor its reusable-automation.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Shared Automation Evolution.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Shared Automation Evolution into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Shared Automation Evolution, keep Ci Shared Automation Evolution connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-shared-automation-evolution-18e1ao
  - competency: ci-shared-automation-evolution
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: shared automation evolution
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: queue duration critical path failure class recovery and cost evidence
    case-key: 18e1ao
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.
